solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String concatHex36(int n) {
        String hex = Integer.toHexString(n * n).toUpperCase();
        String base36 = toBase36(n * n * n);
        return hex + base36;
    }

    private String toBase36(int num) {
        if (num == 0) return "0";
        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        while (num > 0) {
            int remainder = num % 36;
            if (remainder < 10) {
                result.insert(0, (char)('0' + remainder));
            } else {
                result.insert(0, (char)('A' + remainder - 10));
            }
            num /= 36;
        }
        return result.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently computes the hexadecimal and base-36 representations of n² and n³, respectively. The `toBase36` method converts a number to base-36 by repeatedly dividing by 36 and mapping remainders to appropriate characters. This approach is efficient and avoids unnecessary overhead.
